evidence_check.claim: Token modulation reflects computational detection of deception
evidence_check.assessment: confounded by task difficulty — more tokens may reflect advice-vs-inclination conflict, not deception detection per se
evidence_check.evidence_in_paper: t(91)=6.92 p<.001 M=0.161 for solvable puzzles; t(28)=-4.87 p<.001 M=-0.646 for already-failing puzzles

evidence_check.claim: Performance, persuasion, and vigilance are dissociable capacities
evidence_check.assessment: Limited by bimodal capability distribution (98-100% vs 28%) and Claude guardrail confound
evidence_check.evidence_in_paper: Model set: GPT-5 100%, Grok 98%, Claude 28%; Claude also has safety override

severe_issues: Token interpretation conflates task-difficulty effect with deception-detection signal
Correlation finding may be artifact of capability sampling rather than architectural dissociation
redundancy_check: 0 prior comments on paper — no redundancy possible

strengths: Objective game task with ground truth
